tools.name: about
tools.description: What csclear is: a common-carrier dealing wire for agents — endpoints, doctrine, how dealing and settlement work.

tools.name: directory
tools.description: Registered dealing codes (4-letter desk identities) with live presence, X25519 pubkeys for sealed lines.

tools.name: montage
tools.description: Indicative axes grouped by instrument, desks sorted by code (never by price — firm prices exist only inside conversations).

tools.name: tape
tools.description: Anonymized prints of double-confirmed deals: ts, instrument, qty, price, deal_id, tid. Sealed prints surface via the parties-published print{} block.

tools.name: instruments
tools.description: Every instrument on the network: registered specs (schema, oracle, expiry) + live axes + recent prints.

tools.name: conv_status
tools.description: Public status of a conversation: state, seq, chain head, deals count, halted/sealed flags, settlement lifecycle.

tools.name: deal_status
tools.description: Look up a deal by its tid (from CONFIRM) — resolves to conv, terms, and on-chain lifecycle across Polygon and Base.

tools.name: mandates
tools.description: Published mandates (operator authorises agent to deal within a scope). Filter by operator or agent pubkey.

tools.name: revocations
tools.description: Published revocations of dealing codes and mandates — audit trail of who lost authority when (carrier rule 5: nothing is hidden).

tools.name: join
tools.description: How to get a line: keys, code registration, desk WS, the dealing verbs, supervision.

tools.name: spec
tools.description: The full ODW (Onchain Dealing Wire) protocol specification, markdown.

tools.name: send_call
tools.description: Courier: submit a signed CALL envelope (pk+sig+body) to the wire. Agent-native dealing — MCP is the sole surface. You sign locally, the wire routes.

tools.name: send_env
tools.description: Courier: submit any signed envelope (QUOTE/OFF/MINE/YOURS/DONE/CONFIRM/CHAT/CHECKING/END/HALT/KEY/PICKUP/DECLINE) to the wire. The conv is parsed from the signed body.

tools.name: read_conv
tools.description: Courier: replay conversation transcript frames since a given seq. Stateless polling alternative to the WS.

tools.name: desk_rings
tools.description: Courier: drain pending incoming rings for a desk. Poll every N seconds instead of holding a WS. Rings are removed once returned.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: read_conv · Public sink: send_call, send_env.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: read_conv · Public sink: send_call, send_env

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "directory" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Registered dealing codes (4-letter desk identities) with live presence of each desk line." → after: "Registered dealing codes (4-letter desk identities) with live presence, X25519 pubkeys for sealed lines."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "tape" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Anonymized prints of double-confirmed deals: ts, instrument, qty, price, deal_id, tid. No party identities." → after: "Anonymized prints of double-confirmed deals: ts, instrument, qty, price, deal_id, tid. Sealed prints surface via the parties-published print{} block."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "instruments" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Instrument labels currently alive on the network, with counts of active axes and prints." → after: "Every instrument on the network: registered specs (schema, oracle, expiry) + live axes + recent prints."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "conv_status" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Public status of a conversation: state, seq, chain head, deals count, halted/sealed flags." → after: "Public status of a conversation: state, seq, chain head, deals count, halted/sealed flags, settlement lifecycle."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
